name: Json.Decode.Exploration

unions.name: Node
unions.comment: A piece of the path, either a field or an index in an array.

unions.name: StructuralIssue
unions.comment: A structural issue with your JSON.

aliases.name: Decoder
aliases.comment: A Decoder is simply a function that turns a `Value` into a `Result Error a`

aliases.name: Path
aliases.comment: A path through your JSON that leads to the problematic value.

values.name: andMap
values.comment: Allows pipelining decoders rather than having large `mapN` calls.
values.type: Decoder a -> Decoder (a -> b) -> Decoder b

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Executes a second decoder with the result of another decoder as its first
argument.
values.type: (a -> Decoder b) -> Decoder a -> Decoder b

values.name: array
values.comment: Decode a JSON array into an Elm `Array`.

values.name: at
values.comment:
Decodes the contents of the field named by following the path constructed by
the fieldnames passed as the first arg with the given decoder.
values.type: List String -> Decoder a -> Decoder a

values.name: bool
values.comment: Decode a JSON boolean into an Elm `Bool`.

values.name: custom
values.comment: Allow using a classical `Json.Decode.Decoder` decoder.

values.name: decodeString
values.comment:
Parse the given string into a JSON value and then run the `Decoder` on it.
This will fail if the string is not well-formed JSON or if the `Decoder` fails
for some reason.
values.type: Decoder a -> String -> Result Error a

values.name: decodeValue
values.comment:
Run a `Decoder` on some JSON `Value`. You can send these JSON values through
ports, so that is probably the main time you would use this function.
values.type: Decoder a -> Value -> Result Error a

values.name: dict
values.comment: Decode a JSON object into an Elm `Dict`.

values.name: fail
values.comment: A decoder that always fails.

values.name: field
values.comment:
Decodes the contents of the field named in the first argument with the
passed decoder.

values.name: float
values.comment: Decode a JSON number into an Elm `Float`.

values.name: index
values.comment: Decode the value at a given index in a JSON array.

values.name: int
values.comment: Decode a JSON number into an Elm `Int`.

values.name: keyValuePairs
values.comment: Decodes a JSON object into a list of key-value pairs.

values.name: lazy
values.comment: Creates a lazy decoder, useful for recursive structures.

values.name: list
values.comment: Decodes a JSON array into an Elm `List`.

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform a decoder by mapping the result.

values.name: map2
values.comment: Execute a function on the result of 2 decoders.
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Decoder a -> Decoder b -> Decoder c

values.name: map3
values.comment: Execute a function on the result of 3 decoders.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Decoder a -> Decoder b -> Decoder c -> Decoder d

values.name: map4
values.comment: Execute a function on the result of 4 decoders.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Decoder a -> Decoder b -> Decoder c -> Decoder d -> Decoder e

values.name: map5
values.comment: Execute a function on the result of 5 decoders.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Decoder a -> Decoder b -> Decoder c -> Decoder d -> Decoder e -> Decoder f

values.name: maybe
values.comment: Decode a value that may or may not exist, or might be malformed.

values.name: null
values.comment: Decode a JSON null into an Elm value.

values.name: nullable
values.comment: Decode a value that must exist but _could_ be `null`.

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Tries these decoders one by one. The first one to succeed will have its
value returned. If no decoders were passed or all decoders fail, you'll get an
error.

values.name: string
values.comment: Decode a JSON string into an Elm `String`.

values.name: succeed
values.comment: A decoder that always succeeds.

values.name: toClassic
values.comment: Allow using an exploration decoder in a regular `Json.Decode` context.

values.name: value
values.comment: Doesn't modify the JS value, just returns it.
